L1.area: Data sink configuration and JDBC connection URL processing layer

L2.description: A deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability exists where the JDBC connection URL sanitization filter can be bypassed through multi-layered URL encoding. An attacker can craft a connection URL that, after a single decoding pass, still contains dangerous connection parameters that enable automatic deserialization of server-provided objects, potentially leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: MySQL data sink JDBC URL configuration and sanitization handling
L3.description: A deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability exists where the MySQL JDBC connection URL sanitization filter can be bypassed through multi-layered URL encoding. When configuring a MySQL data sink, the URL filtering logic only performs a single decode pass before checking for dangerous connection parameters such as those enabling automatic deserialization. An attacker can double-encode these parameters so they survive the initial decode and reach the JDBC driver intact, enabling deserialization of server-provided objects and potentially leading to remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The JDBC URL sanitization filter only performs a single URL decode pass before checking for dangerous parameters like autoDeserialize. An attacker can double-encode these parameters to bypass the filter, enabling MySQL JDBC deserialization of untrusted server-provided objects.
